verdicts.note: Evidence does not mention any MCP server. Setting 'unknown' is appropriate when no information is found.
Evidence confirms OAuth 2.0 authorization code flow with refresh tokens, and mentions product-limited test tokens for 24-hour trial testing without full OAuth setup.

Evidence confirms admin-approval model: 'Application requests are reviewed each business day' and Standard access requires 'a video recording of your app completing an action using the Pinterest API'.
Evidence confirms: developers register via My Apps → Trial access granted after review → Standard access requires video demo showing OAuth + live integration. No paid tiers mentioned.
Evidence confirms REST API (Pinterest API v5) with OAuth 2.0.
Evidence shows broad coverage: 'Pin Analytics, ads, audiences, boards, Pins, catalogs' and mentions OpenAPI spec. Python SDK is mentioned.
Evidence supports buildable-with-caveats: Trial access is granted after initial review (business days), Standard requires video demo approval. Not instant self-serve, but accessible.
Evidence confirms: 'Application requests are reviewed each business day' for Trial, and Standard requires 'a video recording' for manual review. This matches the stated blocker.
GitHub quickstart repo is confirmed in evidence and supports OAuth examples and client credentials.
Raw GitHub content returned 429 Too Many Requests. Cannot verify OpenAPI spec claim from this URL, though the quickstart repo reference supports its existence.

revised.name: Pinterest
revised.one_liner: Pinterest provides a public REST API (v5) with OAuth 2.0 authentication, requiring app registration and manual approval (Trial then Standard access) rather than instant self-serve keys.

auth_methods.notes: OAuth 2.0 authorization code flow with refresh tokens; product-limited (test) tokens available for 24-hour trial testing without full OAuth setup.

revised.self_serve_notes: Developers register an app via My Apps and are granted free Trial access after business-day review, but must submit a video demo of OAuth + live integration for manual review to reach Standard (production) access. No paid tiers, but approval gating exists.

api_surface.notes: Pinterest API v5 covers Pins, Boards, Ads/Campaigns, Catalogs, Analytics, and more, documented via OpenAPI spec; Python SDK available.

revised.main_blocker: Requires manual app approval (Trial access reviewed each business day, then Standard access review with video demo) before production-level API/OAuth access is granted, which slows self-serve automation.

evidence.supports: OAuth 2.0 flow, app registration, trial access, product-limited tokens, redirect URI setup
Trial vs Standard access tiers and review process
Official quickstart repo with OAuth and API examples, including client credentials support
